commit.message:
Remove unused figures and update acknowledgements

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(browser-demo): clip overlay to video bounds

Summary:
- map overlay vectors into the contained video rectangle instead of the
  full canvas and clip drawing to the visible video area
- switch the browser demo to sample every decoded frame by default and
  compute vectors synchronously to avoid stale frame races
- add regression coverage for contained-video geometry and projected
  vector coordinates

Rationale:
- the overlay canvas was sized to the full viewer card, so vectors could
  extend into letterboxed space when the video used object-fit: contain
- the previous worker pipeline and default stride of 3 made updates look
  sparse and could leave older vectors on newer frames
- low-resolution synchronous estimation is a better fit for this MVP's
  per-frame refresh requirement than an async hop that can miss the
  displayed frame

Tests:
- npm test
- node browser validation probe (desktop/mobile screenshots, rvfc cadence
  check, synthetic clip check)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Fix interactive enable preselection state

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat: implement ars-dom positioning engine (#67) (#116)

Closes #67

Add the pure geometry positioning engine in ars-dom, including:
- compute_position with auto placement, flip, shift, overlap, arrow, and max-size handling
- overflow helpers and boundary resolution for viewport and non-web Boundary::Element fallback
- spec and roadmap synchronization for the shipped contract and follow-up positioning tasks
- expanded tests, coverage cleanup, and explanatory comments for the geometry math

Validation:
- cargo test -p ars-dom positioning
- cargo llvm-cov test -p ars-dom --text -- positioning
- cargo test -p ars-dom
- cargo clippy -p ars-dom --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings
- cargo xci

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Page label views from SQL

Replace eager label message loading with a merged paged query for explicit and sender-labeled messages, and route label load-more requests through the existing infinite scroll path.

Co-authored-by: Codex (GPT-5.4) <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(order): restore variant snapshots and payment finalization (#1134)

* fix(order): restore variant snapshots and payment finalization

* fix(admin): show read-only order item details

* fix(payments): harden verify route and server-side assurance fee

- Do not short-circuit completed transactions when the order is unreconciled;
  fall through so webhook-crash scenarios still finalize the order.
- Add amount/currency verification to the verify route, mirroring the
  webhook's checks, to prevent partial-pay exploits.
- Guard order update behind the transaction claim so concurrent requests
  cannot double-update the order when they lose the race.
- Recompute assurance_fee server-side using a fixed rate constant instead
  of trusting the client-supplied monetary value.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(payments): guard shipping_status regression in verify route

Only advance shipping_status to 'processing' when the order is still
in 'pending' state — prevents regressing orders already moved to
'shipped' or 'delivered'.

Also exclude public/ from Biome lint (static marketing HTML assets,
not application code — noSvgWithoutTitle / useHtmlLang are irrelevant).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: Baci
repository.description: Experiment

commit.message:
feat(pdp): dependent variant filtering — prevent impossible RAM/storage combos (#1138)

* fix(import): delegate to worker instead of inline processing in after()

Replace kickoffImportJob with startImportJob in all 4 import route
after() callbacks (finalize, multipart upload, commit, notify-customers).

startImportJob uses a clean strategy:
- Production with worker secret: delegates to /api/import-jobs/worker
  (runs in its own request context with maxDuration=300)
- Production without secret: throws to catch misconfiguration
- Non-production (localhost): falls back to inline processing

Previously, after() always ran processImportJobById inline first, which
claims the job then does heavy I/O. On Vercel, the function timeout
kills the process mid-work, orphaning the job with no recovery path.

Also adds logger.warn in triggerImportWorker when IMPORT_JOB_WORKER_SECRET
is missing as a defensive layer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(import): harden worker kickoff recovery

* fix(import): only delegate to worker in production + fix test name

* fix(import): only delegate to worker in production environments

* fix(import): delegate to worker whenever secret is configured, not only in production

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(pdp): add dependent variant filtering to prevent impossible combos

Selecting RAM now filters available storage to only valid pairs that
exist in product_variants. Impossible combinations are shown as
disabled with strikethrough styling. Selecting a new axis value
auto-clears any sibling selection that is no longer reachable.

Also consolidates S22–S25 Ultra to ram+storage variant pairs (data
migration run directly in Supabase — no migration file needed as
this was a data backfill, not a schema change).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(pdp): add dependent variant filtering to prevent impossible combos

Selecting RAM now filters available storage to only valid pairs that
exist in product_variants. Impossible combinations are shown as
disabled with strikethrough styling. Selecting a new axis value
auto-clears any sibling selection that is no longer reachable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(test): type onSelectAttribute mock explicitly to satisfy strict TS

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(operator): keep team control plane workspace scoped

Summary:
- stop session-scoping the cockpit team control plane fetch
- preserve request scope while synthesizing control-plane handoff entries
- pin the corrected backend and cockpit contracts in tests

Rationale:
- a review pass found that unscoped control-plane requests could silently drop follow-ups after the approval loop shadowed the request session id
- the cockpit was also presenting a thread-scoped payload as a workspace-level team control plane, which contradicted the shipped contract

Tests:
- python3 -m py_compile backend/src/api/operator.py backend/tests/test_operator_api.py
- cd backend && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_operator_api.py -q
- cd frontend && NODE_OPTIONS=--experimental-require-module npx vitest run src/components/cockpit/CockpitView.test.tsx
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
test(ase-118): sync security settings route expectations

Summary:
- update the security settings HTTP tests so project credential endpoints assert the new
  project-only scope semantics
- stop sending or expecting org scope on project credential save and delete calls

Rationale:
- org credential management now lives on dedicated org routes, so the project route tests were
  asserting an outdated cross-scope contract
- aligning the tests with the routed behavior keeps backend coverage focused on the actual API
  surface we ship

Tests:
- PATH="$PWD/.tooling/go/bin:$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH" go test ./internal/httpapi -run 'TestSecuritySettingsRoute(SavesManualCredential|DeletesCredential)$' -count=1
- PATH="$PWD/.tooling/go/bin:$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH" go test ./internal/httpapi

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
test(ase-118): update github auth stub repository

Summary:
- teach the github auth service test stub the new `GetOrganizationContext` repository method
- derive org test context from the existing project fixture unless a test supplies one explicitly

Rationale:
- the merged org credential feature widened the repository contract used by the github auth
  service, which left the unit-test stub behind
- keeping the stub in sync lets the backend suite validate the real service behavior without
  weakening the interface or skipping coverage

Summary:
- format the updated ticket fixture and org credential files so frontend CI stays clean
- restore the disabled-mode access card copy expected by the IAM migration E2E flow

commit.message:
Add image flip actions in transform mode

Implement horizontal and vertical background image flipping in image
transform mode using H and V shortcuts, backed by the existing undoable
image scale command path. Keep flipped images anchored in place by
recomputing translation while negating xScale or yScale. Add a
state-context FAB row for the new flip actions, make the vertical flip
icon match the horizontal one rotated by 90 degrees, update EN/PT help
and shortcut documentation, and cover both shortcut and UI flows with
regression tests.

Signed-off-by: Rodrigo Severo <rodrigo@mapiah.org>
Co-Authored-By: GPT-5 Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Fix control-point selection during keyboard nudging

Preserve the selected end/control point type when refreshing a selected
line segment after line-point edits. This keeps Arrow-key nudging of a
selected Bézier control point from being rewritten into an end-point
selection after the segment update. Extend the line-point move
regression test to assert that the selected control point type remains
controlPoint1 after keyboard nudging.

Signed-off-by: Rodrigo Severo <rsev@pm.me>
Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: mapiah
repository.description: Graphical interface for cave mapping with Therion written in Flutter.

commit.message:
Add keyboard nudging for selected end/control points

Make selected end/control points in single-line edit mode follow the
same keyboard movement rules as image move mode: Arrow uses
TH2Edit_NudgeFactor, Shift+Arrow uses 10x that value, Alt+Arrow
moves by 1 screen pixel, and Alt+Shift+Arrow moves by 10 screen
pixels. Reuse the shared move-modifier helper for arrow-step
handling, route drag and keyboard line-point moves through shared
controller finalization logic to avoid duplication, and add
regression coverage for end-point and control-point nudge paths.
Update the EN/PT TH2 edit help and keyboard shortcut pages, and
refresh the unreleased changelog entry.

Signed-off-by: Rodrigo Severo <rsev@pm.me>
Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Add keyboard nudging for selected elements

Make selected elements in selection mode follow the same keyboard
movement rules as image move mode: Arrow uses TH2Edit_NudgeFactor,
Shift+Arrow uses 10x that value, Alt+Arrow moves by 1 screen pixel,
and Alt+Shift+Arrow moves by 10 screen pixels. Reuse the shared move
modifier helper for arrow-step calculation, extend delta-based move
commands to selected areas, and add regression coverage for the four
shortcut variants. Update the EN/PT TH2 edit help and keyboard
shortcut pages, and refresh the unreleased changelog entries for
TH2Edit_NudgeFactor.

Signed-off-by: Rodrigo Severo <rsev@pm.me>

commit.message:
Fix: refresh transform handles immediately on mode change

Make image transform overlays repaint when switching between move/scale
and rotate without waiting for mouse movement. Also trigger immediate
selected-element and line-edit redraws when entering and leaving moving
states so selection handles and end/control-point visuals update as
soon as the state changes. Add regression coverage for the image,
moving-elements, and moving-end-point redraw paths.

commit.message:
Fix: repeat arrow-key image nudging reliably

Forward KeyRepeatEvent to the active canvas state and mark arrow keys as
handled in the listener so Flutter focus/navigation does not steal them.
This fixes repeated image nudging in all directions, including ArrowUp,
for both held keys and repeated key presses.

Signed-off-by: Rodrigo Severo <rodrigo@mapiah.com>
Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Deploy browser demo to GitHub Pages (#14)

* feat(deploy): publish browser demo to GitHub Pages

Summary:
- add a GitHub Pages workflow that deploys the static browser demo
  from `browser-demo` on pushes to `main`
- extend the Node test suite with a guard that verifies the Pages
  workflow targets the expected directory and deploy action
- document that the current browser demo is published after merge

Rationale:
- this repo already ships a static browser surface, so Pages can host
  the current review build without introducing a frontend build step
- keeping deployment scoped to `browser-demo` preserves the existing
  local workflow while creating a visible review URL after merge

Tests:
- npm test
- npm run validate:browser-demo

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(validation): use an ephemeral browser demo port

Summary:
- choose a free local port for browser demo validation when no explicit
  port is configured
- bind the temporary static server to 127.0.0.1 so repeated runs use a
  predictable loopback target

Rationale:
- repeated local validation could time out on the fixed default port
  even though the browser demo itself was healthy
- using an ephemeral port keeps the review harness reliable without
  changing the shipped demo surface

Tests:
- npm test
- npm run validate:browser-demo

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Eugene Nikolskiy <4658906+helionaut@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(evolution): add governed self-evolution substrate

Summary:
- add the governed self-evolution engine, API, and native tool for skill, runbook, starter pack, and prompt pack review candidates
- enforce registered-target validation, scope-drift guardrails, and safe workspace contribution persistence with rollback behavior
- add deterministic API and runtime-eval coverage and update shipped-truth docs for Batch AS

Rationale:
- give Seraph a human-reviewed, eval-gated self-evolution path without direct in-place mutation
- fix the real review findings around unregistered file mutation, invalid draft pollution, and under-scoped prompt-pack guardrails

Tests:
- python3 -m py_compile backend/src/api/evolution.py backend/src/evolution/engine.py backend/src/extensions/workspace_package.py backend/src/runbooks/manager.py backend/src/starter_packs/manager.py backend/src/tools/self_evolution_tool.py backend/src/evals/harness.py backend/tests/test_evolution_api.py backend/tests/test_tools_api.py backend/tests/test_eval_harness.py
- cd backend && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_evolution_api.py tests/test_tools_api.py -q
- cd backend && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_eval_harness.py -q -k "governed_self_evolution_behavior or test_main_lists_available_scenarios"
- cd docs && npm run build
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(import): delegate to worker instead of inline processing in after() (#1127)

* fix(import): delegate to worker instead of inline processing in after()

Replace kickoffImportJob with startImportJob in all 4 import route
after() callbacks (finalize, multipart upload, commit, notify-customers).

startImportJob uses a clean strategy:
- Production with worker secret: delegates to /api/import-jobs/worker
  (runs in its own request context with maxDuration=300)
- Production without secret: throws to catch misconfiguration
- Non-production (localhost): falls back to inline processing

Previously, after() always ran processImportJobById inline first, which
claims the job then does heavy I/O. On Vercel, the function timeout
kills the process mid-work, orphaning the job with no recovery path.

Also adds logger.warn in triggerImportWorker when IMPORT_JOB_WORKER_SECRET
is missing as a defensive layer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(import): harden worker kickoff recovery

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add move modifiers for line point dragging

Apply the same drag modifiers used by image and element movement
to single-line end/control point editing. Alt now starts moving
the current point selection even when the drag begins away from
the selected point, Ctrl constrains the move to the dominant
horizontal or vertical axis, and Shift temporarily disables
snapping.

Add regression coverage for end-point and control-point dragging
modifiers and update the changelog entry for the new behavior.

commit.message:
Add image move drag modifiers

Implement modifier-aware image movement in the combined image
move/scale state. Alt now starts moving the selected image even
when the drag begins outside the image body, Ctrl constrains the
move to the dominant horizontal or vertical axis, and Shift
temporarily disables snapping while dragging.

Add regression coverage for the new move modifiers and introduce a
small interaction-key override to keep those tests stable.
